item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The rover was placed inside scp-1312-1? That doesn't make sense. I guess in an earlier version -1 was a location?

And yes, once again, how are we reading the last test log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept of holding up a mirror to the Foundation and showing it at it's most basic really appeals to me. The added horror of not only the fact that the colour people are the lost staff but also what they are doing to those they capture. An all round solid scip +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love this one. Pretty creepy, and the last experiment log is the perfect ending.
+1

item.title: dont you guys think it reeeaaally ironic?
item.content:encoded:
it's like some kind of "god's creation". i mean the god that create everything including scps.

and it is a message to the foundation saying "you'r like a ignorant child that don know sh*t about these things. these scps are only sticks and stones to me. the foundation can do nothing"

it really makes me think…

of couse +1

The Fifth Experiment: It is cut apart with sharp things.
Result: Wet.

AUGH this is the worst thing. thanks! i'm never sleeping again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This didn't really strike the uncanny valley for me, which is what I assume this was trying to hit. However, the idea of something out there impersonating the Foundation and using their means to torture innocent people is somewhat unsettling for me. I can also appreciate the effort that went into this.

+1

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: pastarasta1, you've been making enough silly immature posts lately that people have complained to me about it. Please be more conscientious from now on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NO DAD, YOU WOULDN'T UNDERSTAND MY AWESOME COMEDY!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i don't understand this can you explain it to me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Result: Wet

( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a necro, but this was posted long before the tale in question if I'm reading this right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really understand the chronology of this expedition. I mean, the Agent was lost during the attempted exit. How could the documents recovered during that very expedition include a report about him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get a Grant Morrison Doom Patrol vibe from this, and I like that.

Also, I couldn't help but notice it's Site-41.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. So at the end there is a Foundation operative, who gets cut up and like. Dafuq. Poor man… The SCPF is not an easy Place to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 from me, but I can't work out what the item was being tested in Document 1312-2..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sounds like a Bizzaro universe SCP Foundation. All my +1's
Remember, don't trust Dr. ████. He's a liar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While people (rightly) call this one for being too similar to others, this one stands out in my mind as unique. It's much creepier than Skip(which is just essentially AWCY? pulling a really great prank on the Foundation), doesn't mess with my headcannon quite as much as as that 001 proposal, and frankly is more effective than 1870.

So yeah. Good stuff. The screwy notes at the end, the poor agent's fate, and the fate of whatever those human-like things are got to me. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is like a Dumb or simple, version of the SCP o3o

The 3rd class is D's the First class seems ether O5 and the 2nd class seems to be E's ^_^

SCP RETALIATION!!!!!!!!!! or make a Game and call it "SCP: Site 41 - SCP for Dummies" :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That stuff's way too hazardous already! It's an unregulated chemical found in our tap water that can be used as an industrial solvent.

No warnings about it! And it's everywhere. Of course dihydrogen monoxide is Keter!

Also, necroing a bit there, as that post is over a year old

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dihydrogen Monoxide. Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feels like a lesser version of Skip. "Are We Cool Yet?" did this better.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sums up what I was going to say, just spoken better. It may not be the popular opinion but I like the idea of these twisted mockeries of Foundation staff. That final SCP log nails it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What on earth happened to them? What happened that turned the people into those weird things? Is there something else in the site that the foundation didn't see? Like some kind of object or field?

Whatever it is that made them that way, I wonder what it would do to other groups.

+1, since SOMETHING turned the former personnel into psycho shadows of their former selves, and I want to know what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, especially the "Remember our motto, secure the contain and protect," bit. Gave me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
test: read article
result: +1

item.title: Image Removal
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1312's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Fantem or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

Source:
http://www.subbrit.org.uk/rsg/sites/c/crowborough/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This very unsettling to read. I like it a great deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, the recovered documents are what make this one for me. The idea is interesting, and the "research" carried out by the "personnel" ties it together. I love the sinking feeling you get when you read the third document, knowing in advance what the fifth experiment is going to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wavering between neutral and downvote until the very last document:

And then re-read it with that in my mind. Love it now. I agree with Accelerando, so far it's my favorite of the "Anomalous Foundation" SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember really liking this SCP out of all the other parodies of the Foundation. It's surprisingly simplistic and creepy, not only because of that last line, but because this is what the Foundation really is at its most basic form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed my mind on this one because of the last few lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I remember seeing this in the negatives and waited for its removal, but damn, it's gotten a lot better than last time. Love the idea and the log puts the idea into the spotlight. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This almost feels more like a critique of the Foundation than any alternate or "dumb" version. It reads like how a snobby movie critic would view a Uwe Boll movie. Specifically in terms of characters (flat and one dimensional) and plot (pointless and illogical). My head canon is that a higher level entity or civillization is watching the Foundation and created this SCP as a condescending satire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Fifth Experiment: It is cut apart with sharp things.
Result: Wet.

Result: Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Fifth Experiment: It is cut apart with sharp things.
Result: Wet.

D:

I know there are similar SCP's, but I still find this creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the documentation fit well with the rest of the article. Like you said, they carry out rudimentary versions of Foundation protocols, so would it not also make sense that their documentation was also rudimentary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The footnote is unnecessary. The method of data-gathering just doesn't matter all that much. If nothing else, the reader would assume that it was learned by the penetration team; before the mission went pear-shaped and they lost Agent Banks.

Speaking of which, if the unfortunate Agent Banks was captured on the way out, it is logically impossible for his expedition to have retrieved his experiment log. I'm assuming it was actually colelcted by the second incursion. They didn't succeed in the rescue attempt, but they secured intel that strongly implies he was experimented to death.

Like others, I got a strong deja vu to Skip, but I like the darker spin on the concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only complaint is a minor one based on grammar. "Amount" should be replaced with "number".

item.title: Fast Recovery!
item.content:encoded:
-5 to +38 in less than two days?

Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If there's ever another Five Questions contest, I nominate "What happened to 1312?" to be one of the questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Fifth Experiment: It is cut apart with sharp things.
Result: Wet.

Nice.

This is an excellent take on a somehow twisted small SCP foundation. Reminds me of Skip and the Facechanging SCP community. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This completely encapsulates what I love about this article. The possibilities for why this is is what really makes it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome. This is a wonderful example of the kind of accident I'd expect to have happen within the Foundation. As said earlier in this thread, it's like a certain level of intelligence was retained, but very simplified, same as the groups themselves were simplified to white doctors, black security, and orange d-class. It can't handle anything complex, therefore anything outside of its established parameters is ignored as a lie. The question remains: what happened? As in, not the event itself, but is this the actual members of the site trying to act out their duties, or is it an entity trying to impersonate them, or is it an echo of some kind, or is it a new race entirely that got birthed from the event that caused the site to move and imbued them with these personalities? etc etc. I find it utterly fascinating, even if you hadn't re-written the documentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used to be known as ra_mckay FYI. I still have yet to post a good skip…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anything, I liked it more when I read the additional documentation (Post-edit); before the documentation, I just thought "Oh look, another warped foundation like 1870." Then I got to how they were containing and testing objects obsessively and repetitively, and it just clicked. They're supposed to be simplified to the point of mental deficiency, and anything that falls outside their simple perspective is lies. Works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Information regarding these humanoid entities was gleamed"

Do you mean "gleaned"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This felt a lot like an immune system response. I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the concept, and was about to upvote.then I hit the additional documents.

Sorry, but they just don't seem to do it for me. Something about them seems to lower the quality if the article in my eyes: it feels like it would have been better had ithe article cut off before the documentation was shown.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, that last "result" was perfect. You took that idea and ran with it to a wonderful place. Upvote delivered.

Things I especially like; the crude redaction process, attempting to feed the agent D-class, the idea that "this is what SCPs deserve" as a sort of childish morality, the obsession with the idea of lies and falsehoods. There is real character here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this so hard. The documents remind me of nothing so much as the -J (I think) of fire taking place in the stone age. It's like they shifted sideways in reality and have just a rudimentary memory of how to SCP. It's like they've been reduced to a very basic stage of understanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. It's essentially a primitive, alternate-universe Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It used to be silly before Tanhony fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't see goofy in the documents. I saw simplified. Like they're trying to do what they remember doing, but the complex bits are missing, so they do what they can because they "know" they "must".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am definitely inclined to agree with what has been said. This is a charmless version of of what's been done before, better.

The one nice touch is that they tried to feed the guy D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …better, but still not enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the changes, I changed my downvote to an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. This seems like an amalgamation of several things already on the site (namely Scantron's 001 and James), but for me at least, it lacks their charm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite a bit better, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like these changes.
